225	    def forward(
226	        self,
227	        logits: torch.Tensor,
228	        sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
229	    ) -> Optional[SamplerOutput]:
230	        """
231	        Single-step scheduling:
232	            * Perform GPU-side sampling computation & compute
233	            GPU-side logprobs tensor
234	            * Pythonize sampling result & logprobs tensor
235	
236	        Multi-step scheduling:
237	            * Perform GPU-side sampling computation & compute
238	            GPU-side logprobs tensor
239	            * Defer Pythonization of sampling result & logprobs
240	            tensor
241	            * Encapsulate arguments required for deferred Pythonization
242	            in the
243	            [`SamplerOutput`][vllm.model_executor.layers.sampler.SamplerOutput]
244	            structure
245	
246	        Args:
247	            logits: (num_tokens, vocab_size).
248	            sampling_metadata: Metadata for sampling.
249	        """
250	        assert logits is not None
251	        _, vocab_size = logits.shape
252	
253	        # Prepare sampling tensors with pinned memory to avoid blocking.
254	        if not sampling_metadata.reuse_sampling_tensors:
255	            self._init_sampling_tensors(logits, sampling_metadata)
256	        elif self._do_penalties:
257	            # In this case, the sampling tensors logic depends on
258	            # "output_tokens" of a sequence. As a result, we cannot
259	            # reuse sampling tensors, since "output_tokens" changes
260	            # between decode runs.
261	            self._init_sampling_tensors(logits, sampling_metadata)
262	
263	        assert self._sampling_tensors is not None
264	        sampling_tensors = self._sampling_tensors
265	        do_penalties = self._do_penalties
266	        do_top_p_top_k = self._do_top_p_top_k
267	        do_min_p = self._do_min_p
268	
269	        logits = _apply_min_tokens_penalty(logits, sampling_metadata)
270	
271	        # Apply presence and frequency penalties.
272	        if do_penalties:
273	            logits = apply_penalties(logits, sampling_tensors.prompt_tokens,
274	                                     sampling_tensors.output_tokens,
275	                                     sampling_tensors.presence_penalties,
276	                                     sampling_tensors.frequency_penalties,
277	                                     sampling_tensors.repetition_penalties)
278	
279	        # Use float32 to apply temperature scaling.
280	        # Use in-place division to avoid creating a new tensor.
281	        logits = logits.to(torch.float)
282	        logits.div_(sampling_tensors.temperatures.unsqueeze(dim=1))
283	
284	        if do_top_p_top_k and flashinfer_top_k_top_p_sampling is None:
285	            logits = _apply_top_k_top_p(logits, sampling_tensors.top_ps,
286	                                        sampling_tensors.top_ks)
287	
288	        if do_min_p:
289	            logits = _apply_min_p(logits, sampling_tensors.min_ps)
290	
291	        # We use float32 for probabilities and log probabilities.
292	        # Compute the probabilities.
293	        probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1, dtype=torch.float)
294	        # Compute the log probabilities.
295	        logprobs = torch.log_softmax(logits, dim=-1, dtype=torch.float)
296	
297	        # Sample the next tokens.
298	        maybe_deferred_sample_results, maybe_sampled_tokens_tensor = _sample(
299	            probs,
300	            logprobs,
301	            sampling_metadata,
302	            sampling_tensors,
303	            include_gpu_probs_tensor=self.include_gpu_probs_tensor,
304	            modify_greedy_probs=self._should_modify_greedy_probs_inplace,
305	        )
306	
307	        if self.include_gpu_probs_tensor:
308	            # Since we will defer sampler result Pythonization,
309	            # preserve GPU-side tensors in support of later
310	            # deferred pythonization of logprobs
311	            assert maybe_sampled_tokens_tensor is not None
312	            on_device_tensors = (probs, logprobs, maybe_sampled_tokens_tensor)
313	        else:
314	            # Since Pythonization has already happened, don't preserve
315	            # GPU-side tensors.
316	            on_device_tensors = None
317	
318	        # Get the logprobs query results.
319	        prompt_logprobs = None
320	        sample_logprobs = None
321	        if not sampling_metadata.skip_sampler_cpu_output:
322	            # Pythonize logprobs now (GPU -> CPU); do not defer.
323	            assert not isinstance(maybe_deferred_sample_results,
324	                                  SampleResultArgsType)
325	            prompt_logprobs, sample_logprobs = get_logprobs(
326	                logprobs, sampling_metadata, maybe_deferred_sample_results)
327	
328	        return _build_sampler_output(
329	            maybe_deferred_sample_results,
330	            sampling_metadata,
331	            prompt_logprobs,
332	            sample_logprobs,
333	            on_device_tensors=on_device_tensors,
334	            skip_sampler_cpu_output=sampling_metadata.skip_sampler_cpu_output)

336	    @property
337	    def _should_modify_greedy_probs_inplace(self) -> bool:
338	        """Whether or not the sampler should modify the probability distribution
339	        of greedily-sampled tokens such that multinomial sampling would sample
340	        the greedily-sampled token.
341	
342	        In other words, if True then we set the probability of the greedily-
343	        sampled token to 1.
344	
345	        This is used by speculative decoding, which requires that the sampling
346	        method be encoded into the probability distribution.
347	        """
348	        return self.should_modify_greedy_probs_inplace
349	
350	
351	def _apply_min_tokens_penalty(
352	    logits: torch.Tensor,
353	    sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
354	) -> torch.Tensor:
355	    """Apply min_tokens penalty which sets stop tokens to -inf if min_tokens
356	        have not been generated yet
357	    """
358	    # list of indices in logits that will be set to -inf
359	    logits_to_penalize: list[tuple[int, int]] = []
360	    logits_applied = 0
361	    for seq_group in sampling_metadata.seq_groups:
362	        seq_ids = seq_group.seq_ids
363	        sampling_params = seq_group.sampling_params
364	
365	        sample_indices = seq_group.sample_indices
366	        logits_applied += len(sample_indices) + len(
367	            seq_group.prompt_logprob_indices)
368	        if not seq_group.do_sample:
369	            continue
370	
371	        start_idx = sample_indices[0]
372	        min_tokens = sampling_params.min_tokens
373	        token_ids_to_penalize = sampling_params.all_stop_token_ids
374	        if min_tokens > 0 and token_ids_to_penalize:
375	            seqs_to_penalize: list[int] = []
376	            for j, seq_id in enumerate(seq_ids):
377	                seq_data = seq_group.seq_data[seq_id]
378	                if len(seq_data.output_token_ids_array) < min_tokens:
379	                    seqs_to_penalize.append(j)
380	
381	            if seqs_to_penalize:
382	                # convert to the index into logits
383	                seqs_to_penalize = [start_idx + j for j in seqs_to_penalize]
384	                # itertools.product pairs each seq index with every token id
385	                logits_to_penalize.extend(
386	                    itertools.product(seqs_to_penalize, token_ids_to_penalize))
387	
388	    if logits_to_penalize:
389	        # use zip and * to group indices along each dimension
390	        # eg. [ (1,2), (1,3), (5,6) ] -> ( (1,1,5), (2,3,6) )
391	        logits[tuple(zip(*logits_to_penalize))] = -float("inf")
392	
393	    # verifies that no rows in logits were missed unexpectedly
394	    assert logits_applied == logits.shape[0]
395	    return logits
396	
397	
398	def _apply_top_k_top_p(
399	    logits: torch.Tensor,
400	    p: torch.Tensor,
401	    k: torch.Tensor,
402	) -> torch.Tensor:
403	    logits_sort, logits_idx = logits.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)
404	
405	    # Apply top-k.
406	    top_k_mask = logits_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)
407	    # Get all the top_k values.
408	    top_k_mask = logits_sort.gather(1, top_k_mask.unsqueeze(dim=1))
409	    top_k_mask = logits_sort < top_k_mask
410	    logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
411	
412	    # Apply top-p.
413	    probs_sort = logits_sort.softmax(dim=-1)
414	    probs_sum = probs_sort.cumsum(dim=-1)
415	    top_p_mask = probs_sum <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)
416	    # at least one
417	    top_p_mask[:, -1] = False
418	    logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_p_mask, -float("inf"))
419	
420	    # Re-sort the probabilities.
421	    logits = torch.empty_like(logits_sort).scatter_(dim=-1,
422	                                                    index=logits_idx,
423	                                                    src=logits_sort)
424	    return logits

429	    min_p: torch.Tensor,

433	    https://github.com/oobabooga/text-generation-webui/blob/3146124ec01f02c8fb1650a6517cf1b60b537aaf/modules/sampler_hijack.py#L16C17-L16C17
434	    """

def forward(
        self,
        logits: torch.Tensor,
        sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
    ) -> Optional[SamplerOutput]:
        """
        Single-step scheduling:
            * Perform GPU-side sampling computation & compute
            GPU-side logprobs tensor
            * Pythonize sampling result & logprobs tensor

        Multi-step scheduling:
            * Perform GPU-side sampling computation & compute
            GPU-side logprobs tensor
            * Defer Pythonization of sampling result & logprobs
            tensor
            * Encapsulate arguments required for deferred Pythonization
            in the
            [`SamplerOutput`][vllm.model_executor.layers.sampler.SamplerOutput]
            structure

        Args:
            logits: (num_tokens, vocab_size).
            sampling_metadata: Metadata for sampling.
        """
        assert logits is not None
        _, vocab_size = logits.shape

        # Prepare sampling tensors with pinned memory to avoid blocking.
        if not sampling_metadata.reuse_sampling_tensors:
            self._init_sampling_tensors(logits, sampling_metadata)
        elif self._do_penalties:
            # In this case, the sampling tensors logic depends on
            # "output_tokens" of a sequence. As a result, we cannot
            # reuse sampling tensors, since "output_tokens" changes
            # between decode runs.
            self._init_sampling_tensors(logits, sampling_metadata)

        assert self._sampling_tensors is not None
        sampling_tensors = self._sampling_tensors
        do_penalties = self._do_penalties
        do_top_p_top_k = self._do_top_p_top_k
        do_min_p = self._do_min_p

        logits = _apply_min_tokens_penalty(logits, sampling_metadata)

        # Apply presence and frequency penalties.
        if do_penalties:
            logits = apply_penalties(logits, sampling_tensors.prompt_tokens,
                                     sampling_tensors.output_tokens,
                                     sampling_tensors.presence_penalties,
                                     sampling_tensors.frequency_penalties,
                                     sampling_tensors.repetition_penalties)

        # Use float32 to apply temperature scaling.
        # Use in-place division to avoid creating a new tensor.
        logits = logits.to(torch.float)
        logits.div_(sampling_tensors.temperatures.unsqueeze(dim=1))

        if do_top_p_top_k and flashinfer_top_k_top_p_sampling is None:
            logits = _apply_top_k_top_p(logits, sampling_tensors.top_ps,
                                        sampling_tensors.top_ks)

        if do_min_p:
            logits = _apply_min_p(logits, sampling_tensors.min_ps)

        # We use float32 for probabilities and log probabilities.
        # Compute the probabilities.
        probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1, dtype=torch.float)
        # Compute the log probabilities.
        logprobs = torch.log_softmax(logits, dim=-1, dtype=torch.float)

        # Sample the next tokens.
        maybe_deferred_sample_results, maybe_sampled_tokens_tensor = _sample(
            probs,
            logprobs,
            sampling_metadata,
            sampling_tensors,
            include_gpu_probs_tensor=self.include_gpu_probs_tensor,
            modify_greedy_probs=self._should_modify_greedy_probs_inplace,
        )

        if self.include_gpu_probs_tensor:
            # Since we will defer sampler result Pythonization,
            # preserve GPU-side tensors in support of later
            # deferred pythonization of logprobs
            assert maybe_sampled_tokens_tensor is not None
            on_device_tensors = (probs, logprobs, maybe_sampled_tokens_tensor)
        else:
            # Since Pythonization has already happened, don't preserve
            # GPU-side tensors.
            on_device_tensors = None

        # Get the logprobs query results.
        prompt_logprobs = None
        sample_logprobs = None
        if not sampling_metadata.skip_sampler_cpu_output:
            # Pythonize logprobs now (GPU -> CPU); do not defer.
            assert not isinstance(maybe_deferred_sample_results,
                                  SampleResultArgsType)
            prompt_logprobs, sample_logprobs = get_logprobs(
                logprobs, sampling_metadata, maybe_deferred_sample_results)

        return _build_sampler_output(
            maybe_deferred_sample_results,
            sampling_metadata,
            prompt_logprobs,
            sample_logprobs,
            on_device_tensors=on_device_tensors,
            skip_sampler_cpu_output=sampling_metadata.skip_sampler_cpu_output)

    @property
    def _should_modify_greedy_probs_inplace(self) -> bool:
        """Whether or not the sampler should modify the probability distribution
        of greedily-sampled tokens such that multinomial sampling would sample
        the greedily-sampled token.

        In other words, if True then we set the probability of the greedily-
        sampled token to 1.

        This is used by speculative decoding, which requires that the sampling
        method be encoded into the probability distribution.
        """
        return self.should_modify_greedy_probs_inplace


def _apply_min_tokens_penalty(
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
) -> torch.Tensor:
    """Apply min_tokens penalty which sets stop tokens to -inf if min_tokens
        have not been generated yet
    """
    # list of indices in logits that will be set to -inf
    logits_to_penalize: list[tuple[int, int]] = []
    logits_applied = 0
    for seq_group in sampling_metadata.seq_groups:
        seq_ids = seq_group.seq_ids
        sampling_params = seq_group.sampling_params

        sample_indices = seq_group.sample_indices
        logits_applied += len(sample_indices) + len(
            seq_group.prompt_logprob_indices)
        if not seq_group.do_sample:
            continue

        start_idx = sample_indices[0]
        min_tokens = sampling_params.min_tokens
        token_ids_to_penalize = sampling_params.all_stop_token_ids
        if min_tokens > 0 and token_ids_to_penalize:
            seqs_to_penalize: list[int] = []
            for j, seq_id in enumerate(seq_ids):
                seq_data = seq_group.seq_data[seq_id]
                if len(seq_data.output_token_ids_array) < min_tokens:
                    seqs_to_penalize.append(j)

            if seqs_to_penalize:
                # convert to the index into logits
                seqs_to_penalize = [start_idx + j for j in seqs_to_penalize]
                # itertools.product pairs each seq index with every token id
                logits_to_penalize.extend(
                    itertools.product(seqs_to_penalize, token_ids_to_penalize))

    if logits_to_penalize:
        # use zip and * to group indices along each dimension
        # eg. [ (1,2), (1,3), (5,6) ] -> ( (1,1,5), (2,3,6) )
        logits[tuple(zip(*logits_to_penalize))] = -float("inf")

    # verifies that no rows in logits were missed unexpectedly
    assert logits_applied == logits.shape[0]
    return logits


def _apply_top_k_top_p(
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    p: torch.Tensor,
    k: torch.Tensor,
) -> torch.Tensor:
    logits_sort, logits_idx = logits.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)

    # Apply top-k.
    top_k_mask = logits_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)
    # Get all the top_k values.
    top_k_mask = logits_sort.gather(1, top_k_mask.unsqueeze(dim=1))
    top_k_mask = logits_sort < top_k_mask
    logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_k_mask, -float("inf"))

    # Apply top-p.
    probs_sort = logits_sort.softmax(dim=-1)
    probs_sum = probs_sort.cumsum(dim=-1)
    top_p_mask = probs_sum <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)
    # at least one
    top_p_mask[:, -1] = False
    logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_p_mask, -float("inf"))

    # Re-sort the probabilities.
    logits = torch.empty_like(logits_sort).scatter_(dim=-1,
                                                    index=logits_idx,
                                                    src=logits_sort)
    return logits


def _apply_min_p(
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    min_p: torch.Tensor,
) -> torch.Tensor:
    """
    Adapted from
    https://github.com/oobabooga/text-generation-webui/blob/3146124ec01f02c8fb1650a6517cf1b60b537aaf/modules/sampler_hijack.py#L16C17-L16C17
    """

@dataclass
class SamplingTensors:
    """Tensors for sampling."""

    temperatures: torch.Tensor
    top_ps: torch.Tensor
    top_ks: torch.Tensor
    min_ps: torch.Tensor
    presence_penalties: torch.Tensor
    frequency_penalties: torch.Tensor
    repetition_penalties: torch.Tensor
    prompt_tokens: torch.Tensor
    output_tokens: torch.Tensor

    @classmethod
    def from_sampling_metadata(
        cls,
        sampling_metadata: "SamplingMetadata",
        vocab_size: int,
        device: torch.device,
        dtype: torch.dtype,
    ) -> tuple["SamplingTensors", bool, bool, bool]:
        prompt_tokens: list[array] = []
        output_tokens: list[array] = []
        top_ks: list[int] = []
        temperatures: list[float] = []
        top_ps: list[float] = []
        min_ps: list[float] = []
        presence_penalties: list[float] = []
        frequency_penalties: list[float] = []
        repetition_penalties: list[float] = []
        do_penalties = False
        do_top_p_top_k = False
        do_min_p = False

        assert sampling_metadata.seq_groups is not None
        for seq_group in sampling_metadata.seq_groups:
            seq_ids = seq_group.seq_ids
            sampling_params = seq_group.sampling_params
            temperature = sampling_params.temperature
            p = sampling_params.presence_penalty
            f = sampling_params.frequency_penalty
            r = sampling_params.repetition_penalty
            top_p = sampling_params.top_p
            min_p = sampling_params.min_p

            # k should not be greater than the vocab size.
            top_k = min(sampling_params.top_k, vocab_size)
            top_k = vocab_size if top_k < 1 else top_k
            if temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS:
                # NOTE: Zero temperature means deterministic sampling
                # (i.e., greedy sampling or beam search).
                # Set the temperature to 1 to avoid division by zero.
                temperature = 1.0
            if not do_top_p_top_k and (top_p < 1.0 - _SAMPLING_EPS
                                       or top_k != vocab_size):
                do_top_p_top_k = True
            if not do_min_p and min_p > _SAMPLING_EPS:
                do_min_p = True
            if not do_penalties and (abs(p) >= _SAMPLING_EPS
                                     or abs(f) >= _SAMPLING_EPS
                                     or abs(r - 1.0) >= _SAMPLING_EPS):
                do_penalties = True

            is_prompt = seq_group.is_prompt
            if is_prompt and sampling_params.prompt_logprobs is not None:
                # For tokens in the prompt that we only need to get
                # their logprobs
                query_len = seq_group.query_len
                assert query_len is not None
                prefill_len = len(seq_group.prompt_logprob_indices)
                temperatures += [temperature] * prefill_len
                top_ps += [top_p] * prefill_len
                top_ks += [top_k] * prefill_len
                min_ps += [min_p] * prefill_len
                presence_penalties += [0] * prefill_len
                frequency_penalties += [0] * prefill_len
                repetition_penalties += [1] * prefill_len

            if seq_group.do_sample:
                sample_lens = len(seq_group.sample_indices)
                assert sample_lens >= len(seq_ids)
                temperatures += [temperature] * sample_lens
                top_ps += [top_p] * sample_lens
                top_ks += [top_k] * sample_lens
                min_ps += [min_p] * sample_lens
                presence_penalties += [p] * sample_lens
                frequency_penalties += [f] * sample_lens
                repetition_penalties += [r] * sample_lens

        if do_penalties:
            for seq_group in sampling_metadata.seq_groups:
                seq_ids = seq_group.seq_ids
                sampling_params = seq_group.sampling_params
                if (seq_group.is_prompt
                        and sampling_params.prompt_logprobs is not None):
                    prefill_len = len(seq_group.prompt_logprob_indices)
                    prompt_tokens.extend(
                        array(VLLM_TOKEN_ID_ARRAY_TYPE)
                        for _ in range(prefill_len))
                    output_tokens.extend(
                        array(VLLM_TOKEN_ID_ARRAY_TYPE)
                        for _ in range(prefill_len))
                if seq_group.do_sample:
                    for seq_id in seq_ids:
                        seq_data = seq_group.seq_data[seq_id]
                        prompt_tokens.append(seq_data.prompt_token_ids_array)
                        output_tokens.append(seq_data.output_token_ids_array)

        sampling_tensors = SamplingTensors.from_lists(
            temperatures,
            top_ps,
            top_ks,
            min_ps,
            presence_penalties,
            frequency_penalties,
            repetition_penalties,
            prompt_tokens,
            output_tokens,
            vocab_size,
            device,
            dtype,
        )
        return (sampling_tensors, do_penalties, do_top_p_top_k, do_min_p)

    @classmethod
    def from_lists(
        cls,
        temperatures: list[float],
        top_ps: list[float],
        top_ks: list[int],
        min_ps: list[float],
        presence_penalties: list[float],
        frequency_penalties: list[float],
        repetition_penalties: list[float],
        prompt_tokens: list[array],
        output_tokens: list[array],
        vocab_size: int,
        device: torch.device,
        dtype: torch.dtype,
    ) -> "SamplingTensors":
        # Note that the performance will be very bad without
        # pinned memory.
        pin_memory = is_pin_memory_available()

        do_penalties = prompt_tokens or output_tokens

        if do_penalties:
            prompt_t = make_tensor_with_pad(
                prompt_tokens,
                vocab_size,
                device="cpu",
                dtype=torch.int64,
                pin_memory=pin_memory,
            )
            output_t = make_tensor_with_pad(
                output_tokens,
                vocab_size,
                device="cpu",
                dtype=torch.int64,
                pin_memory=pin_memory,
            )
        else:
            empty_tensor = torch.empty(0, device=device, dtype=torch.long)
            prompt_t = empty_tensor
            output_t = empty_tensor

        temperatures_t = torch.tensor(
            temperatures,
            device="cpu",
            dtype=dtype,
            pin_memory=pin_memory,
        )
        top_ps_t = torch.tensor(
            top_ps,
            device="cpu",
            dtype=dtype,
            pin_memory=pin_memory,
        )
        min_ps_t = torch.tensor(
            min_ps,
            device="cpu",
            dtype=dtype,
            pin_memory=pin_memory,
        )
        presence_penalties_t = torch.tensor(
            presence_penalties,
            device="cpu",
            dtype=dtype,
            pin_memory=pin_memory,
        )
        frequency_penalties_t = torch.tensor(
            frequency_penalties,
            device="cpu",
            dtype=dtype,
            pin_memory=pin_memory,
        )
        repetition_penalties_t = torch.tensor(
            repetition_penalties,
            device="cpu",
            dtype=dtype,
            pin_memory=pin_memory,
        )
        top_ks_t = torch.tensor(
            top_ks,
            device="cpu",
            dtype=torch.int,
            pin_memory=pin_memory,
        )
        # Because the memory is pinned, we can do non-blocking
        # transfer to device.

        return cls(
            temperatures=temperatures_t.to(device=device, non_blocking=True),
            top_ps=top_ps_t.to(device=device, non_blocking=True),
            top_ks=top_ks_t.to(device=device, non_blocking=True),
            min_ps=min_ps_t.to(device=device, non_blocking=True),
            presence_penalties=presence_penalties_t.to(device=device,
                                                       non_blocking=True),
            frequency_penalties=frequency_penalties_t.to(device=device,
                                                         non_blocking=True),
            repetition_penalties=repetition_penalties_t.to(device=device,
                                                           non_blocking=True),
            prompt_tokens=prompt_t.to(device=device, non_blocking=True),
            output_tokens=output_t.to(device=device, non_blocking=True),
        )

@cached_property
    def sampling_type(self) -> SamplingType:
        if self.temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS:
            return SamplingType.GREEDY
        if self.seed is not None:
            return SamplingType.RANDOM_SEED
        return SamplingType.RANDOM

    @property
    def all_stop_token_ids(self) -> set[int]:
        return self._all_stop_token_ids

    @property
    def bad_words_token_ids(self) -> Optional[list[list[int]]]:
        # For internal use only. Backward compatibility not guaranteed
        return self._bad_words_token_ids

    def clone(self) -> "SamplingParams":
        """Deep copy, but maybe not the LogitsProcessor objects.

        LogitsProcessor objects may contain an arbitrary, nontrivial amount of
        data that is expensive to copy. However, if not copied, the processor
        needs to support parallel decoding for multiple sequences
        See https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/issues/3087
        """

        logit_processor_refs = None if self.logits_processors is None else {
            id(lp): lp.clone() if hasattr(lp, 'clone') else lp
            for lp in self.logits_processors
        }
        return copy.deepcopy(self, memo=logit_processor_refs)

    def __repr__(self) -> str:
        return (
            f"SamplingParams(n={self.n}, "
            f"presence_penalty={self.presence_penalty}, "
            f"frequency_penalty={self.frequency_penalty}, "
            f"repetition_penalty={self.repetition_penalty}, "
            f"temperature={self.temperature}, "
            f"top_p={self.top_p}, "
            f"top_k={self.top_k}, "
            f"min_p={self.min_p}, "
            f"seed={self.seed}, "
            f"stop={self.stop}, "
            f"stop_token_ids={self.stop_token_ids}, "
            f"bad_words={self.bad_words}, "
            f"include_stop_str_in_output={self.include_stop_str_in_output}, "
            f"ignore_eos={self.ignore_eos}, "
            f"max_tokens={self.max_tokens}, "
            f"min_tokens={self.min_tokens}, "
            f"logprobs={self.logprobs}, "
            f"prompt_logprobs={self.prompt_logprobs}, "
            f"skip_special_tokens={self.skip_special_tokens}, "
            "spaces_between_special_tokens="
            f"{self.spaces_between_special_tokens}, "
            f"truncate_prompt_tokens={self.truncate_prompt_tokens}, "
            f"guided_decoding={self.guided_decoding}, "
            f"extra_args={self.extra_args})")


class BeamSearchParams(
        msgspec.Struct,
        omit_defaults=True,  # type: ignore[call-arg]
        # required for @cached_property.
        dict=True):  # type: ignore[call-arg]
    """Beam search parameters for text generation."""
    beam_width: int
    max_tokens: int
    ignore_eos: bool = False
    temperature: float = 0.0
    length_penalty: float = 1.0
    include_stop_str_in_output: bool = False

225	class MinPLogitsProcessor(LogitsProcessor):
226	
227	    def __init__(self, max_num_reqs: int, pin_memory: bool,
228	                 device: DeviceLikeType):
229	        super().__init__()
230	        self.min_p_count: int = 0
231	
232	        self.min_p_cpu_tensor = torch.zeros((max_num_reqs, ),
233	                                            dtype=torch.float32,
234	                                            device="cpu",
235	                                            pin_memory=pin_memory)
236	        self.min_p_cpu = self.min_p_cpu_tensor.numpy()
237	        # Pre-allocated device tensor
238	        self.min_p_device: torch.Tensor = torch.empty((max_num_reqs, ),
239	                                                      dtype=torch.float32,
240	                                                      device=device)
241	        # Current slice of the device tensor
242	        self.min_p: torch.Tensor = self.min_p_device[:0]
243	
244	    def is_argmax_invariant(self) -> bool:
245	        """Min-p never impacts greedy sampling"""
246	        return True
247	
248	    def get_min_p_by_index(self, index: int) -> float:
249	        return float(self.min_p_cpu[index])
250	
251	    def update_state(self, batch_update: Optional[BatchUpdate]):
252	        if not batch_update:
253	            return
254	
255	        needs_update = False
256	        # Process added requests.
257	        for index, params, _ in batch_update.added:
258	            min_p = params.min_p if isinstance(params, SamplingParams) else 0.0
259	            if self.min_p_cpu[index] != min_p:
260	                needs_update = True
261	                self.min_p_cpu[index] = min_p
262	            if min_p:
263	                self.min_p_count += 1
264	
265	        if self.min_p_count:
266	            # Process removed requests.
267	            needs_update |= bool(batch_update.removed)
268	            for index in batch_update.removed:
269	                if self.min_p_cpu[index]:
270	                    self.min_p_count -= 1
271	
272	            # Process moved requests, unidirectional (a->b) and swap (a<->b)
273	            for adx, bdx, direct in batch_update.moved:
274	                change = (min_p_a :=
275	                          self.min_p_cpu[adx]) != (min_p_b :=
276	                                                   self.min_p_cpu[bdx])
277	                needs_update |= change
278	                if change:
279	                    self.min_p_cpu[bdx] = min_p_a
280	                    if direct == MoveDirectionality.SWAP:
281	                        self.min_p_cpu[adx] = min_p_b
282	
283	        # Update tensors if needed.
284	        size = batch_update.batch_size
285	        if self.min_p_count and (needs_update or self.min_p.shape[0] != size):
286	            self.min_p = self.min_p_device[:size]
287	            self.min_p.copy_(self.min_p_cpu_tensor[:size], non_blocking=True)
288	            self.min_p.unsqueeze_(1)
289	
290	    def apply(self, logits: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
291	        if not self.min_p_count:
292	            return logits
293	
294	        # Convert logits to probability distribution
295	        probability_values = torch.nn.functional.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
296	        # Calculate maximum probabilities per sequence
297	        max_probabilities = torch.amax(probability_values,
298	                                       dim=-1,
299	                                       keepdim=True)
300	        # Adjust min_p
301	        adjusted_min_p = max_probabilities.mul_(self.min_p)
302	        # Identify valid tokens using threshold comparison
303	        invalid_token_mask = probability_values < adjusted_min_p
304	        # Apply mask using boolean indexing
305	        logits[invalid_token_mask] = -float('inf')
306	        return logits

# SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
# SPDX-FileCopyrightText: Copyright contributors to the vLLM project
import dataclasses
from abc import ABC, abstractmethod
from collections.abc import Iterator, Sequence
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from enum import Enum
from itertools import chain
from typing import Optional, Union

import torch
from torch._prims_common import DeviceLikeType

from vllm import PoolingParams, SamplingParams
from vllm.logger import init_logger

logger = init_logger(__name__)


class MoveDirectionality(Enum):
    # One-way i1->i2 req move within batch
    UNIDIRECTIONAL = 0
    # Two-way i1<->i2 req swap within batch
    SWAP = 1


# (index, params, output_tok_ids) tuples for new
# requests added to the batch.
AddedRequest = tuple[int, Union[SamplingParams, PoolingParams], list[int]]
# (index 1, index 2, directionality) tuples representing
# one-way moves or two-way swaps of requests in batch
MovedRequest = tuple[int, int, MoveDirectionality]
# Batch indices of any removed requests.
RemovedRequest = int


@dataclasses.dataclass(frozen=True)
class BatchUpdate:
    """Persistent batch state change info for logitsprocs"""
    batch_size: int  # Current num reqs in batch

    # Metadata for requests added to, removed from, and moved
    # within the persistent batch.
    #
    # Note: each added request is represented as
    # (index, params, output_tok_ids)
    # Key assumption: output_tok_ids is a reference to the
    # request's running output tokens list; in this way
    # the logits processors always see the latest list of
    # generated tokens
    removed: Sequence[RemovedRequest]
    moved: Sequence[MovedRequest]
    added: Sequence[AddedRequest]


class BatchUpdateBuilder:
    """Helps track persistent batch state changes and build
    a batch update data structure for logitsprocs
    
    Assumptions:

            reqs: list[int] = []
            tok_ids: list[int] = []
            for req, (_, _, stop_tok_ids) in self.min_toks.items():
                reqs.extend([req] * len(stop_tok_ids))
                tok_ids.extend(stop_tok_ids)

            self.logits_slice = (self._device_tensor(reqs, torch.int32),
                                 self._device_tensor(tok_ids, torch.int32))

    def _device_tensor(self, data: list, dtype: torch.dtype) -> torch.Tensor:
        return (torch.tensor(data,
                             device="cpu",
                             dtype=dtype,
                             pin_memory=self.pin_memory).to(device=self.device,
                                                            non_blocking=True))

    def apply(self, logits: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        if self.min_toks:
            # Inhibit EOS token for requests which have not reached min length
            logits[self.logits_slice] = -float("inf")
        return logits


def init_builtin_logitsprocs(pin_memory_available: bool, max_num_reqs: int,
                             device: torch.device) -> LogitsProcessorManager:
    """Construct 'builtin' vLLM logitsprocs which the engine
    loads by default.

    Args:
      pin_memory_available: pinned memory is available for use
                            for use by logitsproc
      max_num_reqs: ceiling on request count in persistent batch
      device: inference device

    Returns:
      Data structure encapsulating loaded logitsprocs
    """
    min_tokens_logitproc = MinTokensLogitsProcessor(
        pin_memory=pin_memory_available, device=device)
    logit_bias_logitproc = LogitBiasLogitsProcessor(
        pin_memory=pin_memory_available, device=device)
    min_p_logitproc = MinPLogitsProcessor(
        pin_memory=pin_memory_available,
        device=device,
        # +1 for temporary swap space
        max_num_reqs=max_num_reqs + 1)
    return LogitsProcessorManager(
        non_argmax_invariant=[
            min_tokens_logitproc,
            logit_bias_logitproc,
        ],
        argmax_invariant=[min_p_logitproc],
    )

def __eq__(self, other: object):
        return isinstance(other,
                          self.__class__) and self.outputs == other.outputs

    def __repr__(self) -> str:
        """Show the shape of a tensor instead of its values to reduce noise.
        """
        sampled_token_probs_repr = ("None" if self.sampled_token_probs is None
                                    else self.sampled_token_probs.shape)
        sampled_token_ids_repr = ("None" if self.sampled_token_ids is None else
                                  self.sampled_token_ids.shape)
        return (
            f"SamplerOutput(outputs={self.outputs}, "
            f"sampled_token_probs={sampled_token_probs_repr}, "
            f"sampled_token_ids={sampled_token_ids_repr}, "
            f"spec_decode_worker_metrics={self.spec_decode_worker_metrics})")


class Sampler(nn.Module):
    """Samples the next tokens from the model's outputs.

    This layer does the following:
    1. Discard the hidden states that are not used for sampling (i.e., all
        tokens except the final one in each prompt).
    2. Compute the logits for the next tokens.
    3. Apply presence, frequency and repetition penalties.
    4. Apply temperature scaling.
    5. Apply top-p and top-k truncation.
    6. Sample the next tokens.
    Here, each sequence group within the batch can have different sampling
    parameters (e.g., sampling method, temperature, top-p, top-k, etc.).

    The structure of the logits tensor is coupled with the seq_groups in
    sampling_metadata. Typically, each sequence in each seq_group has one row in
    logits for the next token to be sampled; however, for a seq_group with a
    prompt request with the prompt_logprobs sampling parameter, there are rows
    in logits for each token in the input prompt.
    """

    def __init__(self):
        super().__init__()

        # Whether or not the SamplerOutput should have on-device tensors
        # containing the sampled token ids and probabilities. This is used by
        # speculative decoding and when prompt embeddings are specified.
        self.include_gpu_probs_tensor = False
        self.should_modify_greedy_probs_inplace = False

    def _init_sampling_tensors(
        self,
        logits: torch.Tensor,
        sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
    ):
        """The goal here is to reuse sampling tensors between similar decode
        runs. This is possible because sampling logic does not change between
        decodes of the same sequences.
        """
        _, vocab_size = logits.shape

        # First free any existing stored sampling tensors.
        # This is necessary because some sampling tensors may
        # have pinned memory.
        self._sampling_tensors = None

        # Initialize new sampling tensors
        (sampling_tensors, do_penalties, do_top_p_top_k,
         do_min_p) = SamplingTensors.from_sampling_metadata(
             sampling_metadata, vocab_size, logits.device, logits.dtype)

        self._sampling_tensors = sampling_tensors
        self._do_penalties = do_penalties
        self._do_top_p_top_k = do_top_p_top_k
        self._do_min_p = do_min_p

    def forward(
        self,
        logits: torch.Tensor,
        sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
    ) -> Optional[SamplerOutput]:

def _apply_min_p(
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    min_p: torch.Tensor,
) -> torch.Tensor:
    """
    Adapted from
    https://github.com/oobabooga/text-generation-webui/blob/3146124ec01f02c8fb1650a6517cf1b60b537aaf/modules/sampler_hijack.py#L16C17-L16C17
    """
    probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
    top_probs, _ = probs.max(dim=-1, keepdim=True)
    scaled_min_p = min_p.unsqueeze_(dim=1) * top_probs
    tokens_to_remove = probs < scaled_min_p
    logits = logits.masked_fill_(tokens_to_remove, -float("inf"))

    return logits


def _greedy_sample(
    selected_seq_groups: list[SequenceGroupToSample],
    samples: torch.Tensor,
) -> SampleResultType:
    """Run greedy sampling on a given samples.

    Args:
        selected_seq_groups: A list of sequence groups batched.
        samples: (num_selected_samples,) A tensor of samples. The length of
            samples could be smaller than selected_seq_groups if
            seq_group.do_sample is False.
    Returns:
        Tuple of (next_token_ids, parent_ids). The length of returned list is
        same as the length of selected_seq_groups. If the corresponding
        seq_group has do_sample=False, tuple contains ([], [])
    """
    samples_lst = samples.tolist()
    sample_idx = 0
    results: SampleResultType = []
    for seq_group in selected_seq_groups:
        if not seq_group.do_sample:
            results.append(([], []))
            continue

        seq_ids = seq_group.seq_ids
        num_parent_seqs = len(seq_ids)
        assert num_parent_seqs == 1, (
            "Greedy sampling should have only one seq.")
        parent_ids = list(range(num_parent_seqs))
        next_token_ids = [samples_lst[sample_idx]]
        results.append((next_token_ids, parent_ids))
        sample_idx += num_parent_seqs
    return results

    selected_seq_groups: list[SequenceGroupToSample],
    random_samples: torch.Tensor,

input.content:
# Plan: Add Top-A Sampling to vLLM

## Context

Top-A sampling keeps only tokens whose probability is at least `a * max_probability`, then renormalizes. This is a probability-threshold-based filtering method (like min_p). When `top_a == 0.0`, filtering is disabled (no-op). The implementation must touch both the V0 (legacy) and V1 (new) sampling paths to maintain feature parity.

**Note:** Top-A (as defined here: `mask where p < top_a * max_p`) is algorithmically identical to min_p. However, the user requests a separate `top_a` parameter, so we implement it as a distinct control.

---

## Files to Modify

| # | File | Purpose |
|---|------|---------|
| 1 | `vllm/sampling_params.py` | Add `top_a` field, validation, docstring, repr |
| 2 | `vllm/v1/sample/logits_processor.py` | Add `TopALogitsProcessor` class (V1 path) |
| 3 | `vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py` | Thread `top_a` through `SamplingTensors` (V0 path) |
| 4 | `vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py` | Add `_apply_top_a()` filtering function (V0 path) |

---

## Step-by-Step

### Step 1: `vllm/sampling_params.py` — Add the `top_a` parameter

**1a. Docstring** (insert after `min_p` docstring, ~line 156):
```
top_a: Float that represents the minimum probability for a token to be
    considered, relative to the maximum probability token. Tokens with
    probability less than top_a * max_probability are filtered out.
    Must be in [0, 1]. Set to 0 to disable this.
```

**1b. Field declaration** (insert after `min_p` on line 214):
```python
top_a: float = 0.0
```

**1c. `from_optional()` method** (~line 261):
- Add `top_a: float = 0.0` parameter
- Pass through to `SamplingParams(... top_a=top_a ...)`

**1d. `__post_init__()` greedy reset** (~line 381):
- Add `self.top_a = 0.0` alongside `self.min_p = 0.0`

**1e. `_verify_args()` validation** (insert after min_p check, ~line 428):
```python
if not 0.0 <= self.top_a <= 1.0:
    raise ValueError("top_a must be in [0, 1], got "
                     f"{self.top_a}.")
```

**1f. `__repr__()`** (~line 572):
- Add `f"top_a={self.top_a}, "` after the `min_p` line

---

### Step 2: `vllm/v1/sample/logits_processor.py` — Add `TopALogitsProcessor`

Model this on `MinPLogitsProcessor` (lines 225-306). Top-A uses the exact same algorithm: mask tokens where `p(token) < top_a * max_p`.

**2a. Create `TopALogitsProcessor` class** (insert after `MinPLogitsProcessor`, before `LogitBiasLogitsProcessor` at ~line 309):

```python
class TopALogitsProcessor(LogitsProcessor):

    def __init__(self, max_num_reqs: int, pin_memory: bool,
                 device: DeviceLikeType):
        super().__init__()
        self.top_a_count: int = 0

        self.top_a_cpu_tensor = torch.zeros((max_num_reqs, ),
                                            dtype=torch.float32,
                                            device="cpu",
                                            pin_memory=pin_memory)
        self.top_a_cpu = self.top_a_cpu_tensor.numpy()
        self.top_a_device: torch.Tensor = torch.empty((max_num_reqs, ),
                                                       dtype=torch.float32,
                                                       device=device)
        self.top_a: torch.Tensor = self.top_a_device[:0]

    def is_argmax_invariant(self) -> bool:
        """Top-A never impacts greedy sampling"""
        return True

    def update_state(self, batch_update: Optional[BatchUpdate]):
        if not batch_update:
            return

        needs_update = False
        for index, params, _ in batch_update.added:
            top_a = params.top_a if isinstance(params, SamplingParams) else 0.0
            if self.top_a_cpu[index] != top_a:
                needs_update = True
                self.top_a_cpu[index] = top_a
            if top_a:
                self.top_a_count += 1

        if self.top_a_count:
            needs_update |= bool(batch_update.removed)
            for index in batch_update.removed:
                if self.top_a_cpu[index]:
                    self.top_a_count -= 1

            for adx, bdx, direct in batch_update.moved:
                change = (top_a_a :=
                          self.top_a_cpu[adx]) != (top_a_b :=
                                                    self.top_a_cpu[bdx])
                needs_update |= change
                if change:
                    self.top_a_cpu[bdx] = top_a_a
                    if direct == MoveDirectionality.SWAP:
                        self.top_a_cpu[adx] = top_a_b

        size = batch_update.batch_size
        if self.top_a_count and (needs_update or self.top_a.shape[0] != size):
            self.top_a = self.top_a_device[:size]
            self.top_a.copy_(self.top_a_cpu_tensor[:size], non_blocking=True)
            self.top_a.unsqueeze_(1)

    def apply(self, logits: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        if not self.top_a_count:
            return logits

        probability_values = torch.nn.functional.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
        max_probabilities = torch.amax(probability_values, dim=-1,
                                       keepdim=True)
        adjusted_top_a = max_probabilities.mul_(self.top_a)
        invalid_token_mask = probability_values < adjusted_top_a
        logits[invalid_token_mask] = -float('inf')
        return logits
```

**2b. Register in `init_builtin_logitsprocs()`** (~line 488):

```python
top_a_logitproc = TopALogitsProcessor(
    pin_memory=pin_memory_available,
    device=device,
    max_num_reqs=max_num_reqs + 1)
```

Add `top_a_logitproc` to the `argmax_invariant` list alongside `min_p_logitproc`:
```python
argmax_invariant=[min_p_logitproc, top_a_logitproc],
```

---

### Step 3: `vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py` — Thread through V0 `SamplingTensors`

**3a. `SamplingTensors` dataclass** (~line 373):
- Add `top_as: torch.Tensor` field

**3b. `from_sampling_metadata()`** (~line 386):
- Add `top_as: list[float] = []` to local variables
- Add `do_top_a = False` flag
- Extract `top_a = sampling_params.top_a` in the loop
- Set `do_top_a = True` when `top_a > _SAMPLING_EPS`
- Append `top_a` values to the list (both prefill and sample paths)
- Pass `top_as` to `from_lists()`
- Return `do_top_a` as part of the tuple (change return to 5-tuple)

**3c. `from_lists()`** (~line 498):
- Add `top_as: list[float]` parameter
- Create `top_as_t` tensor (same pattern as `min_ps_t`)
- Include in returned `cls(...)` constructor

---

### Step 4: `vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py` — Add V0 filtering

**4a. `_init_sampling_tensors()`** (~line 199):
- Unpack `do_top_a` from the now-5-tuple return of `from_sampling_metadata()`
- Store as `self._do_top_a`

**4b. `forward()`** (~line 284):
- After the `do_min_p` block (~line 289), add:
```python
if do_top_a:
    logits = _apply_top_a(logits, sampling_tensors.top_as)
```

**4c. Add `_apply_top_a()` function** (insert after `_apply_min_p`, ~line 441):
```python
def _apply_top_a(
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    top_a: torch.Tensor,
) -> torch.Tensor:
    """Apply Top-A filtering: keep tokens with p >= top_a * max_p."""
    probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
    top_probs, _ = probs.max(dim=-1, keepdim=True)
    scaled_top_a = top_a.unsqueeze_(dim=1) * top_probs
    tokens_to_remove = probs < scaled_top_a
    logits = logits.masked_fill_(tokens_to_remove, -float("inf"))
    return logits
```

---

## Verification

1. **Static checks**: Ensure `top_a` appears in:
   - `SamplingParams` field list, docstring, `__repr__`, `_verify_args`, `__post_init__`
   - V1: `TopALogitsProcessor` class, `init_builtin_logitsprocs`
   - V0: `SamplingTensors`, `from_sampling_metadata`, `from_lists`, `_apply_top_a`, `forward`

2. **Validation test** (manual): Construct `SamplingParams(top_a=0.5)` — should succeed. `SamplingParams(top_a=1.5)` — should raise `ValueError`. `SamplingParams(top_a=-0.1)` — should raise `ValueError`.

3. **Greedy invariance**: `SamplingParams(temperature=0.0, top_a=0.5)` should reset `top_a` to `0.0`.

4. **Grep verification**: After implementation, `grep -r "top_a" vllm/` should show the parameter threaded through all layers consistently.

## Step-by-Step

### Step 1: `vllm/sampling_params.py` — Add the `top_a` parameter

**1a. Docstring** (insert after `min_p` docstring, ~line 156):
```
top_a: Float that represents the minimum probability for a token to be
    considered, relative to the maximum probability token. Tokens with
    probability less than top_a * max_probability are filtered out.
    Must be in [0, 1]. Set to 0 to disable this.
```

**1b. Field declaration** (insert after `min_p` on line 214):
```python
top_a: float = 0.0
```

**1c. `from_optional()` method** (~line 261):
- Add `top_a: float = 0.0` parameter
- Pass through to `SamplingParams(... top_a=top_a ...)`

**1d. `__post_init__()` greedy reset** (~line 381):
- Add `self.top_a = 0.0` alongside `self.min_p = 0.0`

**1e. `_verify_args()` validation** (insert after min_p check, ~line 428):
```python
if not 0.0 <= self.top_a <= 1.0:
    raise ValueError("top_a must be in [0, 1], got "
                     f"{self.top_a}.")
```

**1f. `__repr__()`** (~line 572):
- Add `f"top_a={self.top_a}, "` after the `min_p` line

---

### Step 2: `vllm/v1/sample/logits_processor.py` — Add `TopALogitsProcessor`

Model this on `MinPLogitsProcessor` (lines 225-306). Top-A uses the exact same algorithm: mask tokens where `p(token) < top_a * max_p`.

**2a. Create `TopALogitsProcessor` class** (insert after `MinPLogitsProcessor`, before `LogitBiasLogitsProcessor` at ~line 309):

```python
class TopALogitsProcessor(LogitsProcessor):

    def __init__(self, max_num_reqs: int, pin_memory: bool,
                 device: DeviceLikeType):
        super().__init__()
        self.top_a_count: int = 0

        self.top_a_cpu_tensor = torch.zeros((max_num_reqs, ),
                                            dtype=torch.float32,
                                            device="cpu",
                                            pin_memory=pin_memory)
        self.top_a_cpu = self.top_a_cpu_tensor.numpy()
        self.top_a_device: torch.Tensor = torch.empty((max_num_reqs, ),
                                                       dtype=torch.float32,
                                                       device=device)
        self.top_a: torch.Tensor = self.top_a_device[:0]

    def is_argmax_invariant(self) -> bool:
        """Top-A never impacts greedy sampling"""
        return True

    def update_state(self, batch_update: Optional[BatchUpdate]):
        if not batch_update:
            return

        needs_update = False
        for index, params, _ in batch_update.added:
            top_a = params.top_a if isinstance(params, SamplingParams) else 0.0
            if self.top_a_cpu[index] != top_a:
                needs_update = True
                self.top_a_cpu[index] = top_a
            if top_a:
                self.top_a_count += 1

        if self.top_a_count:
            needs_update |= bool(batch_update.removed)
            for index in batch_update.removed:
                if self.top_a_cpu[index]:
                    self.top_a_count -= 1

            for adx, bdx, direct in batch_update.moved:
                change = (top_a_a :=
                          self.top_a_cpu[adx]) != (top_a_b :=
                                                    self.top_a_cpu[bdx])
                needs_update |= change
                if change:
                    self.top_a_cpu[bdx] = top_a_a
                    if direct == MoveDirectionality.SWAP:
                        self.top_a_cpu[adx] = top_a_b

        size = batch_update.batch_size
        if self.top_a_count and (needs_update or self.top_a.shape[0] != size):
            self.top_a = self.top_a_device[:size]
            self.top_a.copy_(self.top_a_cpu_tensor[:size], non_blocking=True)
            self.top_a.unsqueeze_(1)

    def apply(self, logits: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        if not self.top_a_count:
            return logits

        probability_values = torch.nn.functional.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
        max_probabilities = torch.amax(probability_values, dim=-1,
                                       keepdim=True)
        adjusted_top_a = max_probabilities.mul_(self.top_a)
        invalid_token_mask = probability_values < adjusted_top_a
        logits[invalid_token_mask] = -float('inf')
        return logits
```

**2b. Register in `init_builtin_logitsprocs()`** (~line 488):

```python
top_a_logitproc = TopALogitsProcessor(
    pin_memory=pin_memory_available,
    device=device,
    max_num_reqs=max_num_reqs + 1)
```

Add `top_a_logitproc` to the `argmax_invariant` list alongside `min_p_logitproc`:
```python

```

---

### Step 3: `vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py` — Thread through V0 `SamplingTensors`

**3a. `SamplingTensors` dataclass** (~line 373):
- Add `top_as: torch.Tensor` field

**3b. `from_sampling_metadata()`** (~line 386):
- Add `top_as: list[float] = []` to local variables
- Add `do_top_a = False` flag
- Extract `top_a = sampling_params.top_a` in the loop
- Set `do_top_a = True` when `top_a > _SAMPLING_EPS`
- Append `top_a` values to the list (both prefill and sample paths)
- Pass `top_as` to `from_lists()`
- Return `do_top_a` as part of the tuple (change return to 5-tuple)

**3c. `from_lists()`** (~line 498):
- Add `top_as: list[float]` parameter
- Create `top_as_t` tensor (same pattern as `min_ps_t`)
- Include in returned `cls(...)` constructor

---

### Step 4: `vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py` — Add V0 filtering

**4a. `_init_sampling_tensors()`** (~line 199):
- Unpack `do_top_a` from the now-5-tuple return of `from_sampling_metadata()`
- Store as `self._do_top_a`

**4b. `forward()`** (~line 284):
- After the `do_min_p` block (~line 289), add:
```python
if do_top_a:
    logits = _apply_top_a(logits, sampling_tensors.top_as)
```

**4c. Add `_apply_top_a()` function** (insert after `_apply_min_p`, ~line 441):
```python
def _apply_top_a(
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    top_a: torch.Tensor,
) -> torch.Tensor:
    """Apply Top-A filtering: keep tokens with p >= top_a * max_p."""
    probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
    top_probs, _ = probs.max(dim=-1, keepdim=True)
    scaled_top_a = top_a.unsqueeze_(dim=1) * top_probs
    tokens_to_remove = probs < scaled_top_a
    logits = logits.masked_fill_(tokens_to_remove, -float("inf"))
    return logits
```

---

## Verification

1. **Static checks**: Ensure `top_a` appears in:
   - `SamplingParams` field list, docstring, `__repr__`, `_verify_args`, `__post_init__`
   - V1: `TopALogitsProcessor` class, `init_builtin_logitsprocs`
   - V0: `SamplingTensors`, `from_sampling_metadata`, `from_lists`, `_apply_top_a`, `forward`

2. **Validation test** (manual): Construct `SamplingParams(top_a=0.5)` — should succeed. `SamplingParams(top_a=1.5)` — should raise `ValueError`. `SamplingParams(top_a=-0.1)` — should raise `ValueError`.

3. **Greedy invariance**: `SamplingParams(temperature=0.0, top_a=0.5)` should reset `top_a` to `0.0`.

4. **Grep verification**: After implementation, `grep -r "top_a" vllm/` should show the parameter threaded through all layers consistently.

The plan is ready. Here's a summary of what I found and propose:

**Key architectural insight:** vLLM has two parallel sampling paths — **V0** (legacy, in `model_executor/layers/sampler.py`) and **V1** (new, using `LogitsProcessor` classes in `v1/sample/logits_processor.py`). Top-A needs to be implemented in both.

**4 files to modify:**

1. **`vllm/sampling_params.py`** — Add `top_a: float = 0.0` field, docstring, validation (`0.0 ≤ top_a ≤ 1.0`), greedy reset, and `__repr__`
2. **`vllm/v1/sample/logits_processor.py`** — New `TopALogitsProcessor` class (modeled on the existing `MinPLogitsProcessor` which uses an identical algorithm), registered as an argmax-invariant processor
3. **`vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py`** — Thread `top_a` through `SamplingTensors` (V0 data pipeline)
4. **`vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py`** — Add `_apply_top_a()` function and call it in the V0 `forward()` path

The filtering logic is straightforward: convert logits to probabilities, find max probability per sequence, mask tokens where `p < top_a * max_p`, set those to `-inf`.

# Plan: Add Top-A Sampling to vLLM

## Context

Top-A sampling keeps only tokens whose probability is at least `a * max_probability`, then renormalizes. This is a probability-threshold-based filtering method (like min_p). When `top_a == 0.0`, filtering is disabled (no-op). The implementation must touch both the V0 (legacy) and V1 (new) sampling paths to maintain feature parity.

**Note:** Top-A (as defined here: `mask where p < top_a * max_p`) is algorithmically identical to min_p. However, the user requests a separate `top_a` parameter, so we implement it as a distinct control.

- Store as `self._do_top_a`

**4b. `forward()`** (~line 284):
- After the `do_min_p` block (~line 289), add:
```python
if do_top_a:
    logits = _apply_top_a(logits, sampling_tensors.top_as)
```

**4c. Add `_apply_top_a()` function** (insert after `_apply_min_p`, ~line 441):
```python
def _apply_top_a(
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    top_a: torch.Tensor,
) -> torch.Tensor:
    """Apply Top-A filtering: keep tokens with p >= top_a * max_p."""
    probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
    top_probs, _ = probs.max(dim=-1, keepdim=True)
    scaled_top_a = top_a.unsqueeze_(dim=1) * top_probs
    tokens_to_remove = probs < scaled_top_a
    logits = logits.masked_fill_(tokens_to_remove, -float("inf"))
    return logits
```

2. **Validation test** (manual): Construct `SamplingParams(top_a=0.5)` — should succeed. `SamplingParams(top_a=1.5)` — should raise `ValueError`. `SamplingParams(top_a=-0.1)` — should raise `ValueError`.

3. **Greedy invariance**: `SamplingParams(temperature=0.0, top_a=0.5)` should reset `top_a` to `0.0`.

3	"""Sampling parameters for text generation."""
4	import copy
5	from dataclasses import dataclass

8	from typing import Annotated, Any, Optional, Union
9	
10	import msgspec
11	from pydantic import BaseModel
12	from typing_extensions import deprecated
13	
14	from vllm.logger import init_logger
15	from vllm.logits_process import LogitsProcessor
16	from vllm.transformers_utils.tokenizer import AnyTokenizer
17	
18	logger = init_logger(__name__)
19	
20	_SAMPLING_EPS = 1e-5
21	_MAX_TEMP = 1e-2
22	
23	
24	class SamplingType(IntEnum):
25	    GREEDY = 0
26	    RANDOM = 1
27	    RANDOM_SEED = 2
28	
29	
30	# maybe make msgspec?
31	@dataclass
32	class GuidedDecodingParams:
33	    """One of these fields will be used to build a logit processor."""
34	    json: Optional[Union[str, dict]] = None
35	    regex: Optional[str] = None
36	    choice: Optional[list[str]] = None
37	    grammar: Optional[str] = None
38	    json_object: Optional[bool] = None
39	    """These are other options that can be set"""
40	    backend: Optional[str] = None
41	    backend_was_auto: bool = False
42	    disable_fallback: bool = False
43	    disable_any_whitespace: bool = False
44	    disable_additional_properties: bool = False
45	    whitespace_pattern: Optional[str] = None
46	    structural_tag: Optional[str] = None
47	
48	    @staticmethod
49	    def from_optional(
50	        json: Optional[Union[dict, BaseModel, str]] = None,
51	        regex: Optional[str] = None,
52	        choice: Optional[list[str]] = None,
53	        grammar: Optional[str] = None,
54	        json_object: Optional[bool] = None,
55	        backend: Optional[str] = None,
56	        whitespace_pattern: Optional[str] = None,
57	        structural_tag: Optional[str] = None,
58	    ) -> Optional["GuidedDecodingParams"]:
59	        if all(arg is None for arg in (json, regex, choice, grammar,
60	                                       json_object, structural_tag)):
61	            return None
62	        # Extract json schemas from pydantic models
63	        if isinstance(json, (BaseModel, type(BaseModel))):
64	            json = json.model_json_schema()
65	        return GuidedDecodingParams(
66	            json=json,
67	            regex=regex,
68	            choice=choice,
69	            grammar=grammar,
70	            json_object=json_object,
71	            backend=backend,
72	            whitespace_pattern=whitespace_pattern,
73	            structural_tag=structural_tag,
74	        )
75	
76	    def __post_init__(self):
77	        """Validate that some fields are mutually exclusive."""
78	        guide_count = sum([
79	            self.json is not None, self.regex is not None, self.choice
80	            is not None, self.grammar is not None, self.json_object is not None
81	        ])
82	        if guide_count > 1:
83	            raise ValueError(
84	                "You can only use one kind of guided decoding but multiple are "
85	                f"specified: {self.__dict__}")
86	
87	        if self.backend is not None and ":" in self.backend:
88	            self._extract_backend_options()
89	
90	    @deprecated(
91	        "Passing guided decoding backend options inside backend in the format "
92	        "'backend:...' is deprecated. This will be removed in v0.10.0. Please "
93	        "use the dedicated arguments '--disable-fallback', "
94	        "'--disable-any-whitespace' and '--disable-additional-properties' "
95	        "instead.")
96	    def _extract_backend_options(self):
97	        """Extract backend options from the backend string."""
98	        assert isinstance(self.backend, str)
99	        self.backend, options = self.backend.split(":")
100	        options_set = set(options.strip().split(","))
101	        if "no-fallback" in options_set:
102	            self.disable_fallback = True
103	        if "disable-any-whitespace" in options_set:
104	            self.disable_any_whitespace = True
105	        if "no-additional-properties" in options_set:
106	            self.disable_additional_properties = True
107	
108	
109	class RequestOutputKind(Enum):
110	    # Return entire output so far in every RequestOutput
111	    CUMULATIVE = 0
112	    # Return only deltas in each RequestOutput
113	    DELTA = 1
114	    # Do not return intermediate RequestOutput
115	    FINAL_ONLY = 2
116	
117	
118	class SamplingParams(
119	        msgspec.Struct,
120	        omit_defaults=True,  # type: ignore[call-arg]
121	        # required for @cached_property.
122	        dict=True):  # type: ignore[call-arg]
123	    """Sampling parameters for text generation.
124	
125	    Overall, we follow the sampling parameters from the OpenAI text completion
126	    API (https://platform.openai.com/docs/api-reference/completions/create).
127	    In addition, we support beam search, which is not supported by OpenAI.
128	
129	    Args:
130	        n: Number of output sequences to return for the given prompt.
131	        best_of: Number of output sequences that are generated from the prompt.
132	            From these `best_of` sequences, the top `n` sequences are returned.
133	            `best_of` must be greater than or equal to `n`. By default,
134	            `best_of` is set to `n`. Warning, this is only supported in V0.
135	        presence_penalty: Float that penalizes new tokens based on whether they
136	            appear in the generated text so far. Values > 0 encourage the model
137	            to use new tokens, while values < 0 encourage the model to repeat
138	            tokens.
139	        frequency_penalty: Float that penalizes new tokens based on their
140	            frequency in the generated text so far. Values > 0 encourage the
141	            model to use new tokens, while values < 0 encourage the model to
142	            repeat tokens.
143	        repetition_penalty: Float that penalizes new tokens based on whether
144	            they appear in the prompt and the generated text so far. Values > 1
145	            encourage the model to use new tokens, while values < 1 encourage
146	            the model to repeat tokens.
147	        temperature: Float that controls the randomness of the sampling. Lower
148	            values make the model more deterministic, while higher values make
149	            the model more random. Zero means greedy sampling.
150	        top_p: Float that controls the cumulative probability of the top tokens
151	            to consider. Must be in (0, 1]. Set to 1 to consider all tokens.
152	        top_k: Integer that controls the number of top tokens to consider. Set
153	            to 0 (or -1) to consider all tokens.
154	        min_p: Float that represents the minimum probability for a token to be
155	            considered, relative to the probability of the most likely token.
156	            Must be in [0, 1]. Set to 0 to disable this.
157	        seed: Random seed to use for the generation.
158	        stop: list of strings that stop the generation when they are generated.
159	            The returned output will not contain the stop strings.
160	        stop_token_ids: list of tokens that stop the generation when they are
161	            generated. The returned output will contain the stop tokens unless
162	            the stop tokens are special tokens.
163	        bad_words: list of words that are not allowed to be generated.
164	            More precisely, only the last token of a corresponding
165	            token sequence is not allowed when the next generated token
166	            can complete the sequence.
167	        include_stop_str_in_output: Whether to include the stop strings in
168	            output text. Defaults to False.
169	        ignore_eos: Whether to ignore the EOS token and continue generating
170	            tokens after the EOS token is generated.
171	        max_tokens: Maximum number of tokens to generate per output sequence.
172	        min_tokens: Minimum number of tokens to generate per output sequence
173	            before EOS or stop_token_ids can be generated
174	        logprobs: Number of log probabilities to return per output token.
175	            When set to None, no probability is returned. If set to a non-None
176	            value, the result includes the log probabilities of the specified
177	            number of most likely tokens, as well as the chosen tokens.
178	            Note that the implementation follows the OpenAI API: The API will
179	            always return the log probability of the sampled token, so there
180	            may be up to `logprobs+1` elements in the response.
181	        prompt_logprobs: Number of log probabilities to return per prompt token.
182	        detokenize: Whether to detokenize the output. Defaults to True.
183	        skip_special_tokens: Whether to skip special tokens in the output.
184	        spaces_between_special_tokens: Whether to add spaces between special
185	            tokens in the output.  Defaults to True.
186	        logits_processors: list of functions that modify logits based on
187	            previously generated tokens, and optionally prompt tokens as
188	            a first argument.
189	        truncate_prompt_tokens: If set to -1, will use the truncation size
190	            supported by the model. If set to an integer k, will use only
191	            the last k tokens from the prompt (i.e., left truncation).
192	            Defaults to None (i.e., no truncation).
193	        guided_decoding: If provided, the engine will construct a guided
194	            decoding logits processor from these parameters. Defaults to None.
195	        logit_bias: If provided, the engine will construct a logits processor
196	            that applies these logit biases. Defaults to None.
197	        allowed_token_ids: If provided, the engine will construct a logits
198	            processor which only retains scores for the given token ids.
199	            Defaults to None.
200	        extra_args: Arbitrary additional args, that can be used by custom
201	            sampling implementations, plugins, etc. Not used by any in-tree
202	            sampling implementations.
203	    """
204	
205	    n: int = 1
206	    best_of: Optional[int] = None
207	    _real_n: Optional[int] = None
208	    presence_penalty: float = 0.0
209	    frequency_penalty: float = 0.0
210	    repetition_penalty: float = 1.0
211	    temperature: float = 1.0
212	    top_p: float = 1.0
213	    top_k: int = 0
214	    min_p: float = 0.0
215	    seed: Optional[int] = None
216	    stop: Optional[Union[str, list[str]]] = None
217	    stop_token_ids: Optional[list[int]] = None
218	    ignore_eos: bool = False
219	    max_tokens: Optional[int] = 16
220	    min_tokens: int = 0
221	    logprobs: Optional[int] = None
222	    prompt_logprobs: Optional[int] = None
223	    # NOTE: This parameter is only exposed at the engine level for now.
224	    # It is not exposed in the OpenAI API server, as the OpenAI API does
225	    # not support returning only a list of token IDs.
226	    detokenize: bool = True
227	    skip_special_tokens: bool = True
228	    spaces_between_special_tokens: bool = True
229	    # Optional[list[LogitsProcessor]] type. We use Any here because
230	    # Optional[list[LogitsProcessor]] type is not supported by msgspec.
231	    logits_processors: Optional[Any] = None
232	    include_stop_str_in_output: bool = False
233	    truncate_prompt_tokens: Optional[Annotated[int, msgspec.Meta(ge=1)]] = None
234	    output_kind: RequestOutputKind = RequestOutputKind.CUMULATIVE
235	
236	    # The below fields are not supposed to be used as an input.
237	    # They are set in post_init.
238	    output_text_buffer_length: int = 0
239	    _all_stop_token_ids: set[int] = msgspec.field(default_factory=set)
240	
241	    # Fields used to construct logits processors
242	    guided_decoding: Optional[GuidedDecodingParams] = None
243	    logit_bias: Optional[dict[int, float]] = None
244	    allowed_token_ids: Optional[list[int]] = None
245	    extra_args: Optional[dict[str, Any]] = None
246	
247	    # Fields used for bad words
248	    bad_words: Optional[list[str]] = None
249	    _bad_words_token_ids: Optional[list[list[int]]] = None
250	
251	    @staticmethod
252	    def from_optional(
253	        n: Optional[int] = 1,
254	        best_of: Optional[int] = None,
255	        presence_penalty: Optional[float] = 0.0,
256	        frequency_penalty: Optional[float] = 0.0,
257	        repetition_penalty: Optional[float] = 1.0,
258	        temperature: Optional[float] = 1.0,
259	        top_p: Optional[float] = 1.0,
260	        top_k: int = 0,
261	        min_p: float = 0.0,

265	        bad_words: Optional[list[str]] = None,
266	        include_stop_str_in_output: bool = False,
267	        ignore_eos: bool = False,
268	        max_tokens: Optional[int] = 16,
269	        min_tokens: int = 0,
270	        logprobs: Optional[int] = None,
271	        prompt_logprobs: Optional[int] = None,
272	        detokenize: bool = True,
273	        skip_special_tokens: bool = True,
274	        spaces_between_special_tokens: bool = True,
275	        logits_processors: Optional[list[LogitsProcessor]] = None,
276	        truncate_prompt_tokens: Optional[Annotated[int,
277	                                                   msgspec.Meta(ge=1)]] = None,
278	        output_kind: RequestOutputKind = RequestOutputKind.CUMULATIVE,
279	        guided_decoding: Optional[GuidedDecodingParams] = None,
280	        logit_bias: Optional[Union[dict[int, float], dict[str, float]]] = None,
281	        allowed_token_ids: Optional[list[int]] = None,
282	        extra_args: Optional[dict[str, Any]] = None,
283	    ) -> "SamplingParams":
284	        if logit_bias is not None:
285	            # Convert token_id to integer
286	            # Clamp the bias between -100 and 100 per OpenAI API spec
287	            logit_bias = {
288	                int(token): min(100.0, max(-100.0, bias))
289	                for token, bias in logit_bias.items()
290	            }
291	
292	        return SamplingParams(
293	            n=1 if n is None else n,
294	            best_of=best_of,
295	            presence_penalty=0.0
296	            if presence_penalty is None else presence_penalty,
297	            frequency_penalty=0.0

299	            repetition_penalty=1.0

301	            temperature=1.0 if temperature is None else temperature,
302	            top_p=1.0 if top_p is None else top_p,
303	            top_k=top_k,
304	            min_p=min_p,
305	            seed=seed,
306	            stop=stop,
307	            stop_token_ids=stop_token_ids,
308	            bad_words=bad_words,
309	            include_stop_str_in_output=include_stop_str_in_output,
310	            ignore_eos=ignore_eos,
311	            max_tokens=max_tokens,
312	            min_tokens=min_tokens,
313	            logprobs=logprobs,
314	            prompt_logprobs=prompt_logprobs,
315	            detokenize=detokenize,
316	            skip_special_tokens=skip_special_tokens,
317	            spaces_between_special_tokens=spaces_between_special_tokens,
318	            logits_processors=logits_processors,
319	            truncate_prompt_tokens=truncate_prompt_tokens,

322	            logit_bias=logit_bias,
323	            allowed_token_ids=allowed_token_ids,
324	            extra_args=extra_args,

326	
327	    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
328	        # how we deal with `best_of``:
329	        # if `best_of`` is not set, we default to `n`;
330	        # if `best_of`` is set, we set `n`` to `best_of`,
331	        # and set `_real_n`` to the original `n`.
332	        # when we return the result, we will check
333	        # if we need to return `n` or `_real_n` results
334	        if self.best_of:
335	            if self.best_of < self.n:
336	                raise ValueError(
337	                    f"best_of must be greater than or equal to n, "
338	                    f"got n={self.n} and best_of={self.best_of}.")
339	            if not self._real_n:
340	                self._real_n = self.n
341	                self.n = self.best_of
342	
343	        if 0 < self.temperature < _MAX_TEMP:
344	            logger.warning(
345	                "temperature %s is less than %s, which may cause numerical "
346	                "errors nan or inf in tensors. We have maxed it out to %s.",
347	                self.temperature, _MAX_TEMP, _MAX_TEMP)
348	            self.temperature = max(self.temperature, _MAX_TEMP)
349	
350	        if self.seed == -1:
351	            self.seed = None
352	
353	        if self.stop is None:
354	            self.stop = []
355	        elif isinstance(self.stop, str):
356	            self.stop = [self.stop]
357	
358	        if self.stop_token_ids is None:
359	            self.stop_token_ids = []
360	
361	        if self.bad_words is None:
362	            self.bad_words = []
363	
364	        if self.logprobs is True:
365	            self.logprobs = 1
366	
367	        if self.prompt_logprobs is True:
368	            self.prompt_logprobs = 1
369	
370	        # Number of characters to hold back for stop string evaluation
371	        # until sequence is finished.
372	        if self.stop and not self.include_stop_str_in_output:
373	            self.output_text_buffer_length = max(len(s) for s in self.stop) - 1
374	
375	        self._verify_args()
376	
377	        if self.temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS:
378	            # Zero temperature means greedy sampling.
379	            self.top_p = 1.0
380	            self.top_k = 0
381	            self.min_p = 0.0
382	            self._verify_greedy_sampling()
383	
384	        # eos_token_id is added to this by the engine
385	        self._all_stop_token_ids.update(self.stop_token_ids)
